data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow gain as court strikes down Trump's April tariffs; Nvidia results bring cheer
data.description:
Wall Street major averages pared some gains on Thursday as the rally driven by a court striking down President Trump's April tariffs seemed to fade, but Nvidia's (NVDA) results and guidance continued to lift the mood.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.8%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.45%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 3.95%.

A federal trade court has struck down the “reciprocal” tariffs that Trump imposed on what he called “Liberation Day,” putting an immediate stop to the collection of a sweeping set of levies that the president imposed.

“We should say at the outset that this (the court ruling) is hardly the end of this story, as the administration are appealing this decision. But already, markets have seen a substantial rally in response,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

AI darling Nvidia (NVDA) rose 5.5% after the firm reported fiscal first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, despite the impact of added export control curbs to China.

“The tariff developments have overshadowed the previous day’s news, but sentiment also got a boost from Nvidia’s results after the U.S. close yesterday, as the company delivered upbeat sales guidance,” Allen added.

For now, the court ruling lifts a significant tax burden from US consumers and companies. However, the main outcome is uncertainty, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Looking towards the economic calendar now, there are a few macroeconomic data along with several Federal Reserve speakers lined up on Thursday.

U.S. Q1 GDP decline pared to -0.2% in second estimate.

Initial jobless claims jumped past consensus in the last week, while Q1 U.S. corporate profits dropped 3.6% Q/Q.

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, along with a few other Fed officials, will be speaking later in the day at different times and events. 

Wall Street ended lower Wednesday, with a noticeable downward move in the final hour of trading triggered by a report that the U.S. government had ordered chip designers to stop selling to China.

data.title: HCA, Tenet upgraded at Wells Fargo on receding policy risks for hospitals
data.description:
Wells Fargo on Thursday upgraded HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) and Tenet Healthcare (NYSE:THC), noting that policy risks related to the hospital sector are receding with the House passage of budget reconciliation legislation.

The upgrades came after the House of Representatives voted 215-214 last week to narrowly advance the legislation dubbed "One Big Beautiful Bill," which indicated significant cuts to Medicaid, a healthcare program jointly funded by states and the U.S. government.

“While the legislation will face changes in the Senate and needs to be passed again by the House, we believe healthcare spending cuts are unlikely to worsen and could potentially moderate,” Wells Fargo analyst Stephen Baxter wrote.

The analyst cited coverage impacts as a negative but manageable headwind for the hospital sector, arguing that “negative financial implications from the proposed policies are less severe than we anticipated prior to the release of the initial legislation.”

The analyst noted Universal Health Services (UHS) and Community Health Systems (CYH) as most exposed to the Medicaid proposals and HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) and Tenet Healthcare (NYSE:THC) as least exposed due to their business/payer mix and stronger margins.

Baxter upgraded HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) to Equal Weight from Overweight and raised its price target to $385 from $320 per share. He also upgraded Tenet Healthcare (NYSE:THC) to Overweight from Equal Weight, lifting its per-share target to $195 from $150.

With an Underweight rating, the analyst raised his price target on Community Health Systems (CYH) to $3.50 from $2.00 per share, and with an Overweight rating, he kept his target on Universal Health Services (UHS) unchanged at $225 per share.

data.title: ArcBest appoints Mac Pinkerton as COO of asset-light logistics
data.description:
• ArcBest (NASDAQ:ARCB) on Thursday announced that Mac Pinkerton is joining the company as chief operating officer of asset-light logistics, effective January 5, 2026.
  • Mac succeeds Steven Leonard, who is retiring in June after a 24-year career with ArcBest.
  • The asset-light organization will report to Seth Runser, president of ArcBest, in the interim between Leonard’s retirement and the beginning of January.
  • Shares up 1.9% premarket,

data.title: Keros falls after discontinuation of hypertension treatment trial
data.description:
Keros Therapeutics (NASDAQ:KROS) shares fell on Thursday after the company said it is discontinuing the trial of cibotercept in combination with background therapy in hypertension treatment.

The Keros Board has also decided to reduce the company’s headcount by about 45%.

The company has been under pressure in the recent times, with the stock falling about 70% in the last twelve months. In January, the company halted all dosing in the mid-stage trial of cibotercept in hypertension treatment, including the 1.5 mg/kg and placebo treatment arms, based on safety concerns.

In April, the company had begun a strategic review process for its business and earlier this month, ADAR1 Capital Management, 13.3% shareholder of Keros Therapeutics, also expressed concerns regarding its “prolonged underperformance”.

“The reduction in headcount will commence after the 60-day notice period required by the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act has elapsed, and as a result of these actions, Keros expects to realize average annualized cost savings of approximately $17 million,” the Massachusetts-based company said in a statement.

The stock of the company is down 4% premarket.

data.title: Affirm expands partnership with Williams-Sonoma into Canada, shares up
data.description:
Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) Thursday announced the expansion of its partnership with multichannel retailer Williams-Sonoma (WSM) into Canada, sending shares up 2% in premarket trading. 

This builds on the companies’ multi-year partnership in the U.S. and brings Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) to Canadians shopping at Williams-Sonoma's (WSM) family of brands.

With this launch, Williams-Sonoma (WSM) and its family of brands join leading Canadian retailers, including Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), Brown’s Shoes, and more in offering Affirm’s payment solutions to their customers.

data.title: Civitas Resources announces offering of $500M of senior notes due 2032
data.description:
• Civitas Resources (NYSE:CIVI) said on Thursday that it intends to offer $500M in aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes due 2032 in a private placement. 
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay a portion of the outstanding borrowings under the company’s revolving credit facility.

data.title: C3 AI's contract with U.S. Air Force Rapid Sustainment Office expanded to $450 million, shares rise
data.description:
• C3 AI (NYSE:AI) Thursday said that the United States Air Force (USAF) Rapid Sustainment Office (RSO) has raised the C3 AI contract ceiling to $450 million through October 2029.
  • The additional $350 million in available scope supports the scaling of C3 AI’s predictive analytics and aircraft maintenance platform across the service’s fleet and systems.
  • Initially C3 AI was awarded a $100 million ceiling, which has been fully utilized to deploy PANDA, a predictive maintenance platform.
  • “We believe our program with RSO may be the largest production AI deployment in the U.S. DoD today,” said Ed Abbo, Chief Technology Officer, C3 AI.
  • C3 AI (NYSE:AI) shares up over +16% in premarket trading.

data.title: OSI Systems receives $47M services order
data.description:
• OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) said on Thursday that its Security division received a $47M order from a U.S.-based customer to provide ongoing maintenance service for its installed base of Rapiscan inspection systems that are utilized for screening baggage, cargo and vehicles. 
  • As part of the award, the company is also expected to provide continued support for its CertScan Inspection Integration Platform.

data.title: Hormel's anemic sales weigh on profitability despite price hikes
data.description:
Hormel’s (NYSE:HRL) decision to increase prices last year to offset inflationary pressure on commodity prices was reflected in anemic sales growth for the fiscal second quarter and a decay in profitability, contributing to a 2.5% drop in shares into Thursday’s open.

During the quarter that ended on April 27, Hormel (NYSE:HRL) realized a 0.3% increase in net sales to $2.9B, reflecting a 7% decline in volume and flat sales in both retail and foodservice, both of which were impacted by reduced commodity shipments and, in the case of foodservice, soft industry demand.

While profits were up in certain segments, the company’s overall profit was lower than a year ago to $0.35 per share, a penny better than expected, but 3 cents less than last year.

For FY25, Hormel (NYSE:HRL) now expects net sales to be between $12.0B to $12.2B from prior guidance of $11.9B to $12.2B, less than the $12.11B consensus estimate. Hormel (HRL) also raised the lower end of its organic net sales range to up 2% to up 3% from its prior outlook for 1% to 3% growth.

Adjusted earnings are now expected to be between $1.58 to $1.68 per share from earlier guidance of $1.58 to $1.72 per share, still bracketing the $1.60 estimate.

data.title: Trigon Metals to sell Kombat Project in Namibia to Horizon Corp for $24M in all-cash deal
data.description:
• Trigon Metals (TSXV:TM:CA) has signed a definitive agreement to sell its Kombat project in Namibia to Horizon Corporation and its affiliate Kamino Minerals Limited.
  • The all-cash transaction is valued at $24M.
  • Kamino will acquire 100% of Trigon’s shares in Trigon Ontario and PNT Financeco Corp., as well as the intercompany loan (PNT Loan).
  • The purchase consideration will be paid in eight equal instalments, beginning nine months after shareholder approval and Namibian competition clearance.
  • A pre-closing reorganization will transfer key liabilities and agreements, including the Sprott stream and project-related debt, into Trigon Ontario.
  • Additional cash payments to Trigon may be triggered by underground production milestones, ranging from US$3.5M to US$13M, and a copper royalty of 1% if copper prices exceed $4/lb.
  • Trigon is expected to receive $2M upfront following shareholder approval as part of the total consideration.
  • The Horizon Loan and Additional Loan are restructured in conjunction with the transaction; any project-related debt will be absorbed by the new owner.
  • Source: Press release
